sound_type: piercing scream -- estrus wail 'AAAAEEEEIII'. 500-2,500 Hz. 1-3 seconds. Almost human-sounding. E=7
ecosystem: Forest. Coniferous and mixed forest, lowland to subalpine. Solitary apex predator. Resident year-round.
season: Year-round. Scream peaks in breeding season (December-March).

coordinates.description: Cougar in Cascades forests. Cryptic, solitary, apex predator throughout PNW forested regions.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST FELIDAE. Cat family enters. Solitary predator counterpoint to social Canidae. Apex land predator in PNW.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Puma concolor = 'one-colored cat'. concolor from Latin 'one color'. Also: mountain lion, catamount, panther. Most common names in English.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Puma -- sole living species. Largest wild felid that can purr.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Carnivora. First Felidae entry. Fifth carnivore family in engine.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Felidae: first entry. Counterpoint to Canidae. Solitary vs. social.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Apex land predator. 50-100 kg. Ambush specialist.

song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Scream: 500-2,500 Hz. Growl: 100-500 Hz. Chirp: 1,000-3,000 Hz. Purr: 25-50 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Scream: 1-3 sec. Repeated at 10-30 second intervals.
song_metrics.note_structure: Single sustained scream. Piercing, wavering, drawn-out.
song_metrics.note_types: Estrus scream (piercing, mate attraction), growl (low, threat), chirp/whistle (mother-kitten, bird-like), purr (contentment, subsonic).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Single screams with long pauses. No chorus (solitary). Breeding season.
song_metrics.timbre: Piercing, wavering, drawn-out. Overtone-rich. Almost human-sounding. Terrifying.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=7. Piercing. Forest-penetrating. Same energy as wolf but cuts through canopy rather than filling valleys.
song_metrics.tempo: Screams every 10-30 seconds during estrus bouts. Rare outside breeding season.
song_metrics.context: Estrus (female mate attraction). Breeding season December-March. Rarely heard.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal variation across range. The scream is consistent.
song_metrics.key_property: COUGAR SCREAM. Piercing wail. 500-2,500 Hz. E=7. FIRST FELIDAE. Apex land predator. Ghost cat. Estrus function. v1.49.353.

pelage.overall: Tawny/golden-brown coat. 50-100 kg. Long body (1.5-2.4 m nose to tail). Small rounded ears. Long heavy tail.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Large tawny cat. Long heavy tail (often held low). Small rounded ears. No spots (adults). Powerful build.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Minimal. Coat slightly thicker in winter. Color consistent year-round.

physical_description.overall: 50-100 kg (110-220 lbs). Body length 1.0-1.5 m plus 60-90 cm tail. Shoulder height 60-76 cm. Fourth largest cat in world.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Large tawny cat with long heavy tail. No spots (adults). Small head relative to body. Powerful hindquarters for leaping. Retractable claws.
physical_description.field_id: Large tawny cat, rarely seen. Long tail, powerful build. If you see one, it chose to be seen. Maintain eye contact, appear large, do not run. Note: you are far more likely to find sign (tracks, scrapes, kills) than the animal itself.

nodes.title: FIRST FELIDAE -- Cat Family Enters
nodes.description: 41 species. Retractable claws. Solitary hunters. The cougar is the PNW's apex land predator. Counterpoint to social Canidae.

nodes.title: The Scream -- Estrus and Terror
nodes.description: Piercing wail for mate attraction across 50-400 km2 territories. Overlaps human distress frequencies. Triggers primal freeze response. 1-3 km through forest.

nodes.title: Ghost Cat -- Cryptic Apex Predator
nodes.description: 50-100 kg moving unseen through human areas. Ambush specialist: stalk, sprint, neck bite. Proximity fear: the predator you know is there but never see.

nodes.title: Wolf vs. Cougar at E=7 -- Two Strategies
nodes.description: Wolf fills space (low frequency, pack chorus, valleys). Cougar cuts through space (high frequency, solo scream, forest). Same energy, opposite acoustics.

cross_references.relationship: TWO E=7 APEX VOICES! Wolf fills valleys (low). Cougar cuts forest (high). Same energy, opposite strategies. Pack vs. solo.
CANIDAE TO FELIDAE! Fox bark (E=4) to cougar scream (E=7). +3 leap. Family transition. Social to solitary.
PREDATOR SEQUENCE! Energy arc 5-4-7-4-7. Two E=7 peaks: wolf and cougar. Two apex voices.
SOUND FROM THE DARK! Dream whisper (E=4) and forest scream (E=7). Dream darkness and forest darkness.
